comment: FR-SPD-002 within-clip freeze: the curve ramps at 8x for 1/4s (keyframes 0->0, 1/4->2), then holds source time 2 (frame 192 of 96 fps frame-index-coded media) until 3/2. Sampling t=1/2 inside the zero-slope span must decode source frame 192. The paired time-remap-freeze-hold-late fixture samples t=1/1 against byte-identical reference content, which is the held-frame guarantee. Dropping the curve (1x) decodes frame 48 and flattening it to its constant-rate chord (4/3x) decodes frame 64 - measured failures maxDeltaE=78.5 and 74.6 against this reference. Tolerance 10 is ~2x the measured cross-machine ProRes/color-conversion noise floor (maxDeltaE 4.614 on the CI runner vs 0.000 locally) and every wrong-variant signal is >= 2x the tolerance; it is tighter than the repo's usual decode-path 25 so the suite-wide weakest FR-SPD-002 signal (22.2 on time-remap-ramp-quad) stays discriminating.
